task.title: Setting local build options for smart logical COBOL and PL/I files
task.shortdesc: By default, bidirectional-enabled projects use a setting that converts source and executable files to visual layout.

taskbody.context: This setting can cause errors if a source file contains an invisible LRM marker. When the following string is included in a COBOL program, for example, the compiler issues an error message: IGYGR1056, which indicates that the literal exceeds the length that is specified:Literal declaration that contains bidirectional charactersAlthough the literal declaration is visually correct, the compiler counts the invisible LRM marker and issues the error message.

steps.stepsection: To prevent this type of error, set local build options as follows:

step.cmd: Open the Local COBOL Build Options or Local PL/I Build Options properties page.
In the Environment Variables field, type the set statement set bidiInput=Logical.
step.stepresult: This statement prevents your source from being converted to visual format before compilation.
